explanations.8: This checks if the requested file doesn't actually exist on disk — the !-f means 'not a file'. If it doesn't exist, the next rule will fire.
explanations.9: This sends all requests that matched the previous condition to index.html, with QSA preserving the query string and L stopping further rule processing.
explanations.29: Same as line 8 — checking that the requested file doesn't exist before redirecting to index.html.
explanations.30: Same as line 9 — catch-all redirect for the HTTPS side that sends missing files to index.html with query strings intact.
